summary:
The patient has an increased risk of developing severe or life-threatening bacterial infections such as those from pneumococcus, haemophilius, and meningitis organisms post-splenectomy because their ability to fight these specific types of microbes diminishes significantly without proper immunological support provided by the spleen. This heightened vulnerability underscores why prophylactic vaccinations are crucial before surgery if possible. \boxed{B}
